assignment_description:
Stage 1: CAECILIUS (Nominative Singular Nouns) and Cerberus
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook, sticky notes

PREP: Read "Introduction," “Model sentences,” and “Cerberus” Teacher's Manual p.18-23.

➜ INTRO
This is the story of a real person who lived in Pompeii. What do you know about Pompeii? Look at the picture on the front cover; this is Caecilius. What kind of person do you think he might have been?

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.14 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 1." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 1.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences p.2-5

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Cerberus" p.6
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. What might this story be about? Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 1 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

➜ DISCUSS
How is this family alike and different from yours?
Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER TIP
The translations for all the model sentences and stories are in the digital Cambridge Unit 1 Teacher's Resource document, "Student's Book Translations and Answers." The answers to "About the language" and "Practicing the language" exercises are also located here. This resource is part of the digital Teacher's Manual. The document can be downloaded as a single file containing all the Unit 1 translations and answers or as individual files for each stage.

assignment_description:
Stage 1: Cerberus and Caecilius
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “About the language” and “Cultural context material: Caecilius” Teacher's Manual p.23-25.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 1 as you read the stories in Stage 1.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences p.2-5

➜ PRACTICE
Copy Model Sentences 1-4 on p.2-3 into Latin notebook. Translate into English and back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
"Cerberus" p.6 and “Caecilius” p.8-9
Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language" p.7

➜ PRACTICE
Find sentences with similar patterns in “Model Sentences” or "Cerberus."

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 1 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 1: Metella and Houses of Pompeii
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Practicing the language," “Cultural context material: Metella," and “Cultural context material: Houses in Pompeii” Teacher's Manual p.23, 25-26.

➜ RECAP
• “About the language” p.7
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Metella” and “Houses in Pompeii” p.10-13

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercises 1 and 2 of "Practicing the language" on p.7.

➜ DISCUSS
How is this family alike and different from yours?

Stage 1: Cerberus
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate lines 1-8 of “Cerberus” on p.6 into English.

➜ DISCUSS
How is this family alike and different from yours?

Stage 2: IN VĪLLĀ (Nominative and Accusative Singular Nouns) and Metella
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook, sticky notes

PREP: Read "Opening page" and “Model sentences” Teacher's Manual p.29-30.

➜ INTRO
Look at the picture on p.15. How does this home resemble and differ from homes today? What happens at your house when company comes over? In the Model Sentences on p.18-19, Metella plays hostess.

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.26 with sticky notes. Copy “Vocabulary Checklist 2." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 2.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences ("amīcus”) p.16-17

➜ DISCUSS
In which tense are all these sentences written? (present)

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences ("Metella”) p.18-19

➜ DISCUSS
Name the subject of each of the sentences on p.18-19. (coquus, Metella, Grumiō, Metella, cibus, Metella, Grumiō, Metella, amīcus, & Metella)

➜ DICTATE
Study Model Sentences 11 and 19 on p.18-19. Write sentences from dictation when ready.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 2 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 2: Mercātor and About the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “mercātor” and “About the language” Teacher's Manual p.30-31, 32.

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.
• Model Sentences p.16-19

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 2 as you read the stories in Stage 2.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“mercātor” p.20
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. What might this story be about? Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

• Observe the endings of "Caecilium," "vīllam," "mercātōrem," and "tablīnum" in the first paragraph. The endings are different from what students are used to (Caecilius, vīlla, mercātor, and tablīno).
• Find any other words in the story that have similar endings and speculate why they might be different.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language” p.21

➜ PRACTICE
• Using p.21 as a reference, write a narration explaining why word endings change in the nominative and accusative cases.
• Reread “mercātor” on p.20 and notice how word endings (nominative/accusative cases) affect the translation.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 2 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 2: Practicing the Language and In Triclīniō
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “in triclīniō” and "Practicing the language" Teacher's Manual p.31-33.

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.
• Review the nominative and accusative cases of nouns on p.21.

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercises 1 and 2 of "Practicing the language" on p.22. Then choose two sentences to translate back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 2 as you read the stories in Stage 2.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“in triclīniō” p.20
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 2 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

➜ DISCUSS
Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 2: In Triclīniō and Practicing the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.
• Review the nominative and accusative cases of nouns on p.21.
• “in triclīniō” p.20

➜ PRACTICE
Look for nouns in their nominative and accusative forms in the story “in triclīniō” on p.20.

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercise 3 of "Practicing the language" on p.22. Then choose two sentences to translate back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes. (Note: the student has two lessons to complete this activity.)

➜ EVALUATE
Are all words from Vocabulary Checklist 2 (p.26) correctly defined in Latin notebook?

Stage 2: Practicing the Language and Daily Life
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Cultural context material” Teacher's Manual p.33-34.

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.
• Review the nominative and accusative cases of nouns p.21.

➜ PRACTICE
Finish Exercise 3 of "Practicing the language" on p.22. Then choose two sentences to translate back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Daily life” p.23-24

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
Look at the pictures and captions on p.24-25.

Stage 2: In Triclīniō
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Stage 1 and Stage 2 in Latin notebook.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate “in triclīniō” on p.20 into English. (The student has two lessons to complete this activity.)

Stage 2: In Triclīniō and Review
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Stage 1 and Stage 2 in Latin notebook.

➜ PRACTICE
Finish translating “in triclīniō” on p.20 into English.

➜ CATCH UP
Complete any missed activities from Stage 1 or Stage 2. If you are caught up, practice translating Model Sentences into English and then back into Latin, or use this extra time for another subject.

Stage 3: NEGŌTIUM (Nominative and Accusative of 1st, 2nd, & 3rd Declensions) and Artifex
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, sticky notes, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “in forō” and “artifex” Teacher's Manual p.36-38.

➜ INTRO
• Look at the title of Stage 3 on p.27. What do you think it means? With what kinds of people would you negotiate? This stage is about several types of business dealings.
• Look at the pictures that accompany the story on p.29. What might this story be about?

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.38 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 3." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 3.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“in forō” p.28
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“artifex” p.29
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read aloud and narrate lines 1 to 8. Read silently and narrate lines 9 to the end.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate lines 9-15 of "artifex" on p.29 into English. Translate two sentences back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals and correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 3 or the glossary beginning on p.193.
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER TIP
The translations for all the model sentences and stories are in the digital Cambridge Unit 1 Teacher's Resource document, "Student's Book Translations and Answers." The answers to "About the language" and "Practicing the language" exercises are also located here. This resource is part of the digital Teacher's Manual. The document can be downloaded as a single file containing all the Unit 1 translations and answers or as individual files for each stage.

assignment_description:
Stage 3: Tōnsor and Vēnālīcius
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “tōnsor" and "vēnālīcius" Teacher's Manual p.38-41.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
Look at the picture that accompanies the story on p.30. What might this story be about?

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings from Vocabulary Checklist 3 as you read the stories in Stage 3.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“tōnsor” p.30
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate in small sections.

➜ PRACTICE
Write the answers to the questions that accompany the story “tōnsor” on p.30 in Latin. Pay attention to spelling, capitalization, and accent marks.

➜ DICTATE
Study the last sentence of “tōnsor” on p.30. Write the sentence from dictation when ready.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“vēnālīcius” p.31
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. What might this story be about? Read and narrate in small sections.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate the last paragraph of “vēnālīcius” on p.31 into English and then back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 3 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

➜ DISCUSS
Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 3: About the Language and The Town of Pompeii
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “About the language,” "Practicing the language," and "Cultural context material" Teacher's Manual p.41-44.

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.
• Review the nominative and accusative cases of nouns on p.21.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language” p.32

➜ PRACTICE
Using p.32 as a reference, write a narration explaining the following terms and how you can tell them apart: nominative, accusative, first declension, second declension, and third declension. Check your narration against the student text and make any corrections necessary.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“The town of Pompeii” p.33-34 "The town of" - "convenient crossing places."

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercise 1 of "Practicing the language" on p.33. Then choose one sentence to translate back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

Stage 3: The Town of Pompeii and Vēnālīcius
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
Study the map of Pompeii on p.34.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“The town of Pompeii” p.35 "In all the main" - "and religion."

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercise 2 of "Practicing the language" on p.33. Then choose one sentence to translate back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

➜ RECAP
Look at the picture and recall the story of “vēnālīcius” on p.31.

➜ READ & PRACTICE
“vēnālīcius” p.31
Reread the story aloud. Translate lines 11-17 into English. Translate the last sentence back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Are all words from Vocabulary Checklist 3 (p.38) correctly defined in Latin notebook?

Stage 3: Ad and Nōn
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
• What do the following words have in common: advise, adventure, and adverb? What does "ad" mean in Latin? (to)
• What do the following words have in common: nonsense, nondairy, and nonexistent? What does "nōn" mean in Latin? (not)

➜ PRACTICE ("ad")
• Write a definition of these words that includes the Latin meaning of "ad": advise, adventure, adverb.
• Write a list of English words that include the Latin prefix "ad" and their meanings. Use a dictionary to help you.

➜ PRACTICE ("nōn")
• Write a definition of these words that includes the Latin meaning of "nōn": nonsense, nondairy, nonexistent.
• Write a list of English words that include the Latin prefix "nōn" and their meanings. Use a dictionary to help you.

➜ PRACTICE ("portat")
If there is time left, write a list of English words derived from the Latin word "portat," which means carries.

➜ PRACTICE ("magnus")
If there is time left, write a list of English words derived from the Latin word "magnus," which means big, large, or great.

Review and Catch Up
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3 in Latin notebook.

➜ CATCH UP
Complete any missed activities from Stage 1, Stage 2, and Stage 3. If you are caught up, practice translating Model Sentences into English and then back into Latin, or use this extra time for another subject.

Stage 4: IN FORŌ (Interrogative Words, 1st & 2nd Person Singular Present) and Model Sentences
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, sticky notes, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Model sentences” Teacher's Manual p.45-46.

➜ INTRO
Look at the picture on p.39. What kinds of activities do you think took place in the forum?

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.52 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 4." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 4.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences 1-6 p.40

➜ PRACTICE
• Look again at sentences 1-6 on p.40. What do they have in common? (first person: ego; ego sum)
• Make up similar sentences to describe yourself; use the glossary if needed.

➜ RECAP
Review the meanings of "ego" and "ego sum." (I, I am)

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences 7-15 p.41-42

➜ PRACTICE
• Look again at sentences 7-12 on p.41. What do they have in common? (quid tu; ego)
• Review the meanings of first, second, and third person in English. (I, you, he/she/it)
• Notice the word endings in sentences 7-12. How do they differ?

➜ PRACTICE (1st and 2nd person singular present)
• Write a chart of first and second-person word endings in the sentences on p.40-42.
• For example, #7: 1st person: ego coquō (I cook); 2nd person: tu coquīs (You cook)

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 4 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 4: Hermogenēs and In Basilicā
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Hermogenēs” and “in basilicā” Teacher's Manual p.46-48.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 4 as you read the stories in Stage 4.

➜ INTRO
• Look at the picture that accompanies the story on p.43. Imagine what the buildings looked like before they fell into ruin. How are they similar to and different from the shops we have today?
• What was a basilica in Ancient Rome? (a court of law)
• Knowing that a basilica was a court of law, what kinds of things would you expect to happen in this story?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Hermogenēs” and “in basilicā” p.43-44
Preview vocabulary words that accompany each story. Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜READ & PRACTICE
Reread “Hermogenēs” on p.43, using appropriate inflection. Copy the last paragraph. Translate it into English and then back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 4 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 4: About the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “About the language” Teacher's Manual p.48.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language” paragraphs 1-4 p.45
Read and narrate after each section. Review "being verbs" in English (e.g., I am, you are, he/she/it is) and compare to Latin.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate each sentence in paragraph 5 on p.45 into English; watch out for implied subjects. Choose two sentences to translate back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ DICTATE
Study the dialogue of Lūcia and Caecilius in sentence 8 on p.41. Write their dialogue from dictation when ready.

➜ DISCUSS
Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 4: The Forum and Practicing the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.
• “About the language” p.45

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“The forum” p.48-50

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercise 1 of "Practicing the language" on p.46. Then choose two sentences to translate back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

Stage 4: Practicing the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Stages 1-3 in Latin notebook.
•"About the language” p.45

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercise 2 of "Practicing the language" on p.47. Translate lines 1-12 of "Grumiō et leō" into English. Then translate the last sentence back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Forum - focus of life” p.50-51

➜ EVALUATE
Are all words from Vocabulary Checklist 4 (p.52) correctly defined in Latin notebook?

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to course.

Questions will come from:
Cambridge Unit 1
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Latin 1 (Independent Study) — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 1: Tell whether each of the following words is in the nominative or accusative case: pecūniam, ancilla, amīcus, canem.

Answers: accusative, nominative, nominative, accusative

· Cambridge Unit 1: Translate the first paragraph of "Cerberus" on p.6 into English. (Cover the vocabulary words at the top and bottom of the page.) Then close your book and translate your English paragraph back into Latin.

assignment_description:
Stage 5: IN THEĀTRŌ (Nominative Plural, 3rd Person Plural Present) and In Viā
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, sticky notes, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Model sentences” Teacher's Manual p.53-54.

➜ INTRO
Do you have an amphitheater in your town, or have you ever visited one? What does it look like? Compare it to the ancient amphitheater in the picture on p.65.

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.68 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 5." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 5.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences: "in viā" p.54-55

• Reread sentences 1-2. What is the difference between them? (1 is singular and 2 is plural)
• Notice the word endings and est/sunt.

➜ PRACTICE
Copy Model Sentences 1-10 on p.54-55. Translate them into English and then back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 5 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

The document can be downloaded as a single file containing all the Unit 1 translations and answers or as individual files for each stage.

assignment_description:
Stage 5: The Theater at Pompeii
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Cultural context material” Teacher's Manual p.57-58.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“The theater at Pompeii” p.64-67

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 5 as you read the stories in Stage 5.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences: "in theātro" p.56-57

➜ PRACTICE
Copy Model Sentences 11-20 on p.56-57. Underline the word endings that indicate that the nouns and verbs are plural. Translate the sentences into English and then back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 5 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

➜ DISCUSS
Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 5: Āctōrēs and Poppaea
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Āctōrēs,” “About the language 1,” and “Poppaea” Teacher's Manual p.54-56.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Latin notebook.

➜ INTRO
• Look at the mask on the title page on p.53 and the picture of actors that accompanies the story, āctōrēs, on p.58. How are the costumes similar to and different from those found in plays and movies today?
• Look at the picture of the actor studying his mask on p.60. What do you suppose he is thinking?

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 5 as you read the stories in Stage 5.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“āctōrēs” p.58
Preview vocabulary words that accompany this story. Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ PRACTICE
Reread “āctōrēs” on p.58. Translate the last two sentences into English and then back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Poppaea” p.61
Preview vocabulary words that accompany this story. Read and narrate the dialogue in segments.

➜ PRACTICE
Reread the dialogue on p.61 aloud, taking parts and using appropriate inflection.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language 1” p.59-60

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 5 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 5: About the Language 2 and Practicing the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

• Review “About the language 1” p.59-60.
• Review nominative and accusative cases p.21.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language 2” p.62

➜ PRACTICE
On p.62, translate the sentences in paragraph 5 into English and then back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ PRACTICE

Stage 5: Practicing the Language and In Theātrō

• Review the nominative singular and plural in the three declensions on p.62.

➜ PRACTICE

• Preview the vocabulary words that accompany Exercise 3. Translate Exercise 3 into English; use the glossary only when necessary.

Stage 6: FĒLĪX (Perfect and Imperfect Tenses), About the Language, and Pugna
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, sticky notes, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “pugna” and “About the language” Teacher's Manual p.62-64.

➜ INTRO
Look at the pictures that accompany the model sentences on p.70-71. What might this story be about?

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.82 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 6." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 6.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences p.70-71

★ Latin 1 (Independent Study) — TERM 2 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 1: Translate into Latin: 
a. I am angry.
b. You walk.
c. The slave sits.

Answers:
a. Ego sum iratus.
b. Tū ambulās.
c. Servus sedet.

· Cambridge Unit 1: Translate the first paragraph of Hermogenēs on p.43 into English. (Cover the vocabulary words at the bottom of the page.) Then close your book and translate your English paragraph back into Latin.

Complete Exercise 1 of “Practicing the language" on p.142. Then translate two sentences back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Are all words from Vocabulary Checklist 10 (p.146) correctly defined in Latin notebook?
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER TIP
The translations for all the model sentences and stories are in the digital Cambridge Unit 1 Teacher's Resource document, "Student's Book Translations and Answers." The answers to "About the language" and "Practicing the language" exercises are also located here. This resource is part of the digital Teacher's Manual. The document can be downloaded as a single file containing all the Unit 1 translations and answers or as individual files for each stage.

assignment_description:
Review and Catch-Up Day
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words from Stage 9 and Stage 10 in Latin notebook.

➜ CATCH UP
Complete any missed activities from Stage 9 or Stage 10. If you are caught up, translate Model Sentences into English and then back into Latin, or use this extra time for another subject.

Stage 11: CANDIDĀTĪ (Intransitive Verbs, Questions) and Quīntus et Lūcia
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook, sticky notes

PREP: Read “Model sentences” and “Quīntus et Lūcia” Teacher's Manual p.99-101.

➜ INTRO
Look at the title and picture at the beginning of this stage on p.147. What might this person be doing?

➜ WRITE
Cover the definitions on p.162 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 11." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 11.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Model Sentences p.148-149

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Quīntus et Lūcia” p.150
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate the last four lines of “Quīntus et Lūcia” on p.150 into English.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 11 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 11: Sulla and Local Government and Elections
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “Sulla” Teacher's Manual p.101.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Local government and elections” p.159-160 "The Pompeians took" - "for ten years."

• How is our election season the same and different?

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 11 as you read the stories in Stage 11.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Sulla” p.151
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate the story.

➜ PRACTICE
Translate the last paragraph of “Sulla” on p.151 into English.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 11 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

➜ DISCUSS
Which words from today's lesson look or sound like words you know in English, Spanish, or French?

Stage 11: About the Language 1 and In Vīllā
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “About the language 1,” “Lūcius Spurius Pompōniānus,” and “in vīllā” Teacher's Manual p.101-102.

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 11 as you read the stories in Stage 11.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Election notices” p.160-161

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language 1” p.152

➜ PRACTICE & DICTATE
• Translate the examples in paragraph 4 on p.152 into English.
• Study sentence b. Write from dictation when ready.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“in vīllā p.153
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate the dialogue in small sections.

➜ PRACTICE
Reread the dialogue of “in vīllā” on p.153 aloud, taking parts and using appropriate inflection. Translate lines 21-23 into English.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 11 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 11: Prope Amphitheātrum and In Forō
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “prope amphitheātrum” and "in forō" Teacher's Manual p.102-103.

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.
• Review what has happened in the story so far (see “in vīllā” p.153).

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 11 as you read the stories in Stage 11.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“prope amphitheātrum” and “in forō” p.154-155
Review vocabulary words that accompany each story. Read and narrate the dialogue in small sections.

➜ PRACTICE
Reread the dialogue of “prope amphitheātrum” on p.154 aloud, taking parts and using appropriate inflection. Translate lines 4-6 (Grumiō's first lines) and 10-11 into English.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 11 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 11: In Culīnā, About the Language 2, and Practicing the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

PREP: Read “in culīnā” and “About the language 2” Teacher's Manual p.103-104.

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.
• Review what has happened in the stories so far (see “prope amphitheātrum” and “in forō” p.154-155).

➜ WRITE
Continue to infer and record meanings in Vocabulary Checklist 11 as you read the stories in Stage 11.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“in culīnā” p.155
Review vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate the dialogue in small sections.

➜ PRACTICE & DICTATE
• Reread the dialogue of “in culīnā” on p.155 aloud, taking parts and using appropriate inflection. Translate line 2 into English (Clēmēns' first line).
• Study line 2. Write from dictation when ready.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language 2” paragraph 1 p.156

➜ PRACTICE & DICTATE
• Translate the examples in paragraph 2 on p.156 into English.
• Study example j. Write from dictation when ready.

➜ PRACTICE & DICTATE
• Complete Exercises 1 and 2 of "Practicing the language" on p.157. Translate two sentences back into Latin. Check your translations against the originals; correct any mistakes.
• Study line a of Exercise 1. Write from dictation when ready.

➜ EVALUATE
Are all words from Vocabulary Checklist 11 (p.162) correctly defined in Latin notebook?

Stage 12: VESUVIUS (1st & 2nd Person Singular and Plural, Imperfect and Perfect) and Tremōrēs

Cover the definitions on p.178 with sticky notes. Copy "Vocabulary Checklist 12." Infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 12.

Model Sentences p.164-165

“tremōrēs” p.166
Preview vocabulary words that accompany the story. Read and narrate each paragraph separately.

➜ PRACTICE
Reread “tremōrēs” on p.166 aloud, using appropriate inflection. Translate lines 1-2 into English and then back into Latin. Check your translation against the original; correct any mistakes.

➜ EVALUATE
Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 12 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 12: Ad Urbem and Ad Vīllam
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

Check any new vocabulary words you defined today with Vocabulary Checklist 12 or the glossary beginning on p.193.

Stage 12: Fīnis and About the Language
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“The rediscovery of Pompeii and Herculaneum” p.177 "At Herculaneum," - "2,000 years ago."

Continue to infer and record meanings as you read the stories in Stage 12.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“About the language” paragraphs 1-4 p.172

Stage 12: Nouns, Verbs, and Catch-Up
⍞ Materials: Cambridge Unit 1, Latin notebook

➜ RECAP
• Review Vocabulary Checklist words in Latin notebook.
• Review “About the language” p.172-173.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Nouns” paragraphs 1-3 p.182-183

➜ PRACTICE
Complete Exercise 5 on p.183.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
“Verbs" paragraphs 1-5 p.184-185

➜ PRACTICE

➜ CATCH UP
Use any extra time to complete missed activities from Stages 11 or 12.

Exam Week

Answer question(s) related to course.

Cambridge Unit 1
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Latin 1 (Independent Study) — TERM 3 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Cambridge Unit 1: Translate into Latin:
a. The slaves are working. 
b. The slaves were working. 
c. The slaves worked.

Answers:
a. servī labōrant.
b. servī labōrābant.
c. servī labōrāverunt.

· Cambridge Unit 1: Translate the first paragraph of “Fēlīx” on p.72 into English. (Cover the vocabulary words.) Then close your book and translate your English paragraph back into Latin.
